slides.notes: CV cover. Magazine-editorial layout — dark sidebar with avatar, main area with tagline and four stat blocks. Mina Seo, staff researcher at a compute-intensive AI lab, five years in ML infrastructure. No clicks. Pause on the stats: 7+ years shipping, 3 papers, 40% perf gain, 12x iteration speedup.

elements.alt: Illustrated portrait of Mina Seo — long wavy black hair, warm smile, charcoal blazer over cream blouse, editorial magazine style

elements.content: Five years building inference infrastructure at the intersection of physics simulation and large-scale ML training.

slides.notes:
About section. On enter: PROFILE tag, title, pull-quote in sage green ("Happiest at the boundary where math meets systems"), two body paragraphs auto-reveal.

[step:1]"CURRENTLY INTERESTED IN" tag + interests line — mixed-precision scheduling · sparse attention kernels · reproducibility in stochastic training · Rust for scientific pipelines.[/step]

This is the conversation-hook slide — audience picks an interest and asks about it.

elements.content: Happiest at the boundary where math meets systems:
CUDA kernels, distributed schedulers, reproducibility tooling.

Engineer with a background in computational physics and a track record of shipping low-level ML infrastructure. Currently staff researcher at Compute Forge AI, where I lead the numerical-stability workstream for large-scale pretraining.

Prior work spans molecular dynamics of battery electrolytes, atmospheric ensemble pipelines at a climate lab, and distributed fine-tuning platforms. I'm especially drawn to problems where throwing more GPUs at the issue won't work — you have to do the math.
Mixed-precision scheduling · sparse attention kernels · reproducibility in stochastic training · Rust for scientific pipelines

slides.notes:
Experience timeline. On enter: EXPERIENCE tag, title "Selected roles", vertical sage-green axis (dots hidden until clicks).

[step:1]2024–present · Staff Researcher at Compute Forge AI — leads numerical-stability workstream for large-scale pretraining, shipped mixed-precision loss-scaling scheduler to three production frontier models.[/step]

[step:2]2021–2024 · ML Platform Engineer at NorthBay Systems — built Kubernetes+Ray distributed training platform, cut fine-tuning iteration from 2 days to 4 hours.[/step]

[step:3]2019–2021 · Research Engineer at Meridian Climate Labs — prior: UCSC CMP physics grad 2017–19.[/step]

Read each role's one-line achievement aloud — those are the talking points.

elements.content: Leads the numerical-stability workstream for large-scale pretraining. Shipped a mixed-precision loss-scaling scheduler now used across three production frontier models. Authored the post-mortem discipline for silent NaN regressions.

Built a distributed training platform on Kubernetes + Ray that cut fine-tuning iteration time for the ranking team from two days to four hours. Shipped the checkpointing layer used by every production pipeline.
Meridian Climate Labs  ·  prior: UC Santa Cruz graduate researcher (CMP physics, 2017–19)

slides.notes:
Project spotlight. On enter: SELECTED PROJECTS tag, title "Things I've built".

[step:1]01 Gradient Atlas — diagnostic viz for mixed-precision training, open-source 2.3k stars (Rust · CUDA · WGPU).[/step]

[step:2]02 StormCast-Lite — distilled forecasting model on single GPU (PyTorch · Triton · MPI).[/step]

elements.content: Diagnostic visualization for mixed-precision training dynamics. Open-source; 2.3k stars.
Distilled forecasting model running on a single GPU. Used by two research groups for rapid ensemble prototyping.
Reproducible environment specs for scientific ML — pins the entire CUDA/BLAS/Python stack against a single Nix flake.

slides.notes:
Closing — availability + contact. On enter: LET'S TALK tag + gold rule + title + italic subtitle + body paragraph (team prefs, no on-call love, yes code-lives-for-years love).

[step:1]Dark contact card on the right slides in: REACH OUT header in sage + email + website + GitHub in cream.[/step]

Close warm: "happy to relocate for the right problem."

elements.content: Open to staff and principal-level roles
*in systems-ML, infrastructure research, or scientific computing.*
Bias toward small, highly-technical teams. Comfortable remote; happy to relocate for the right problem. I don't love on-call rotations, I do love writing code that runs in production for years.
